programmes.show_description: Alf is feeling the effects of the demon drink. Else is unsure whether it was the single glass of port or the sausages that made him ill. S5 E6: Party Night
With Boomtown Rats and Suzi Quatro. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
Simon's holiday to a Greek village is interrupted when a mysterious visitor returns from America and The Saint must step in when they cause trouble for the locals. S2 E24: Sophia

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
After Lindbergh's fame, his baby was kidnapped in 1932. Carpenter Hauptmann was executed, but mysteries remain. Narrated by Robert Powell.
Examining the history of war tactics. Flank attack - going around the side of the enemy - is a basic manoeuvre, yet stories of victories decided by flanking fill military textbooks. S2 E4
From supersonic spy planes to sophisticated satellites, get an insider's look at the pioneers of aerial reconnaissance and the profound impact they have had on modern warfare. S2 E4: Spies in the Sky
The F-15 Eagle immediately turned the tide of the first Gulf War to prove its greatness, and was involved in one of the most bizarre combat incidents during that conflict. S2 E6
An exploration of London's underground railway and skyline, the origins of Metro-land - London's vast suburbia - and the clever marketing that tempted millions to move outside the city. Ep4
We look at the British Armoured Division, known as the Desert Rats, who conducted an epic campaign against Rommel's Axis forces in the deserts of Egypt and Libya. Ep4: Desert Rats
Tim Pigott-Smith narrates the story of the Soviet victory over the mighty Japanese Kwangtung Army in the mountains of Manchuria, which is one of the least known of the Second World War.
Ordered to attack the St Nazaire U-boat base, British commandos undertook 'the greatest raid of all' by turning an old destroyer into a live bomb to ram the gates. Ep4: Storm at St. Nazaire
Giant aircraft carriers dominated the Pacific War between Japan and the USA - Pearl Harbor was launched to destroy US carriers but failed. Ep4: Aircraft Carriers
The Supermarine Spitfire aircraft begged the question of how important air combat is in WWII - it has been clear that dominating the skies is essential in battle since then. E4: Pure Fighter
A look at how mechanised warfare aided Germany's advance into France and the way commando raids, blitzkrieg, mobile desert warfare and amphibious landings changed warfare. Ep1: Sudden Attack
With Suzi Quatro and Joe Jackson. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E7: A Pair Of Bloomers
October 1908: there is a thief in the house, but who is the culprit? Is it Sarah, a visiting friend of James, or perhaps something more organised than just petty theft? S1 E11: The Swedish Tiger
Arthur is only too willing to help when local eccentric Albert comes into an inheritance - but his help isn't necessarily needed. S6 E6: Waiting For Goddard
The duo probe the connections between a murdered antiques dealer and an Arab embassy official with diplomatic immunity after a robbery. S2 E6: Blood Money
Alex thinks she's close to death and has to keep her brain alive by solving a raid at a Post Office; Gene believes the culprit is Chas Cale, a blagger he once crossed. S1 E6
Alf is concerned to find that his neighbour's house has been sold to the Council for social housing. S5 E7
With Cliff Richard and Dave Edmunds and Rockville. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
Three charming elderly ladies have a secret that might be about to be exposed, but Simon detests blackmail. S2 E25: The Gentle Ladies

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
In 1937 the Nazi Hindenburg airship burst into flames. New film raised doubts. Tragic accident or sabotage? Narrated by Robert Powell.
Examining the history of war tactics. Special operations are the cutting edge of warfare. Undertaken by elite personnel, they can cover everything from intelligence gathering to raiding. S2 E5
Learn about newly-discovered documents that revealed Stalin's influence over China's Mao Tse Tung and Korea's Kim El Sung, plus other factors behind the Cold War. S2 E5: Behind the Bamboo Curtain
The Hurricane will be remembered for the vital role it played in WWII, and how it destroyed more enemy aircraft during the Battle of Britain than all other air and ground defences combined. S3 E1
Winston Churchill was a steadfast leader throughout World War II; we trace his footsteps from his birth at Blenheim Palace in Oxfordshire to his early military and eventual political career.
After escaping the German invasion of their homeland in 1939, Polish fighter pilots and ground forces played a key role in the liberation of western Europe. Ep5: The Free Polish Forces
Tim Pigott-Smith narrates the story of one of Second World War's most harrowing episodes - the 890-day siege of the Soviet Union's second city Leningrad which was not liberated until January 1944.
Raw materials essential to keep the Nazis fighting came through Bordeaux before Royal Marine Major 'Blondie' Hasler sent a special raiding force in canoes. Ep5: Cockleshell Raiders
The Allied fronts in both western and eastern Europe had become faster-moving by 1944 - a new form of aircraft was needed to deliver swift powerful attacks against German lines. Ep5: Fighter Bombers
Artillery hasn't really changed; once the foundation of battle in WWII, it appears as though artillery isn't going anywhere until political and moral issues regarding accuracy arise. E5: Gods of War
A look at how the battles of World War II increased in sheer size of the forces involved, the power of their weapons, and Germany's strategies for Operation Barbarossa. Ep2: Overwhelming Force
With The Police. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
Cindernellie: Hit '70s sitcom with Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker. Nellie and Eli are left performing a pantomime after the producer does a runner with their money. Christmas 1972 (S7, E1)
Winter 1908: with her 21st birthday imminent, Elizabeth appears to be heading for a falling-out with her parents as a consequence of her actions. S1 E12: The Key of the Door
Terry leaves his fingerprints on a stolen lorry while doing a job for Arthur, then panics at the prospect of retribution. S7 E1: It's a Sorry Lorry Morrie
When a top model is murdered by a hit-and-run driver, Dempsey and Makepeace discover that she had connections at the highest levels. S2 E7: Set a Thief
Drama series following the exploits of Life on Mars DCI Gene Hunt. When thousands of pounds of charity money is stolen, Gene resorts to his 'fists first, questions later' method. S1 E7
